entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The primary crop studied, Sorghum bicolor L. moench, focused on for grain production in drylands

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area, used as a measure of crop performance

entities.name: RATIO OF YIELD TO EVAPOTRANSPIRATION
entities.description: A measure of water-use efficiency calculating the grain yield relative to water lost through evapotranspiration

entities.name: SYSTEM ANALYSIS
entities.description: An analytical approach used to identify plant traits that can be altered to improve yield

entities.name: MOISTURE-LIMITED ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: Environments characterized by limited available water resources affecting crop growth

entities.name: APSIM MODEL
entities.description: A crop simulation model used to simulate sorghum growth and development over time

entities.name: KANO, NIGERIA
entities.description: A study location in Nigeria characterized by high rainfall variability

entities.name: BAMAKO, MALI
entities.description: A study location in Mali characterized by high rainfall variability

entities.name: SUDANO-SAHELIAN REGION
entities.description: The geographical region in West Africa where the study was conducted

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The broader region encompassing the Sudano-Sahelian area

entities.name: LEAF SIZE
entities.description: A plant morphological trait adjusted in the APSIM model to observe effects on yield

entities.name: SEED GROWTH
entities.description: A plant developmental trait adjusted in the APSIM model to observe effects on yield

entities.name: CLIMATE VARIABILITY
entities.description: Fluctuations in climate patterns that necessitate adaptation strategies for crop varieties

entities.name: SOIL QUALITY
entities.description: The health and nutrient status of the soil, which needs to be maintained for crop improvement

entities.name: SEMIARID ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Regions with low rainfall where dryland crops are typically grown
